name: Canal de Castilla – Grijota Loop from Palencia
description: This moderate 15.0-mile (24.1 km) mountain biking route near Palencia offers rural landscapes, historic bridges, and 656 feet of elevation g
region_intro: Mountain biking around Palencia offers diverse terrain across its varied landscapes. The region features a striking contrast between the rugged peaks of the Montaña Palentina in the north and the flat plains of Tierra de Campos to the south. Mountain bike trails in Palencia include high mountain routes with significant elevation gains, as well as less technical paths along the historic Canal de Castilla and riverbanks. This provides a range of options for different skill levels and preferences. Best mountain bike trails around Palencia The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque de Valdellano — circular desde Palencia, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the park's varied terrain near the city. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Palencia a Husillos — circular por Canal de Castilla, a moderate 27.4 miles (44.0 km) path. This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, offering scenic views along its towpaths. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo de Monzón de Campos — circular desde Palencia por el Castillo de Sarmiento, a 20.9 miles (33.6 km) trail leading through the plains and past historical landmarks, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Mountain biking around Palencia is defined by high mountain routes in the Montaña Palentina, flat paths along the Canal de Castilla, and rolling hills in Tierra de Campos. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Palencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Palencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Palencia?
A: Palencia offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 35 routes available for exploration. These range from easy rides to more challenging ascents, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Palencia?
A: The terrain in Palencia is quite diverse. You'll find rugged peaks and significant elevation gains in the Montaña Palentina to the north, offering challenging routes. In contrast, the historic Canal de Castilla provides mostly flat paths ideal for less technical rides, while the Tierra de Campos region features rolling hills and vast fields.

Q: What is the best time to visit for mountain biking in Palencia?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Palencia generally depends on the specific area. The high mountain routes in Montaña Palentina are best enjoyed from late spring to early autumn to avoid snow and harsh winter conditions. For the flatter routes along the Canal de Castilla or in Tierra de Campos, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, though summer can also be suitable, especially in the mornings or evenings.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Palencia?
A: Yes, Palencia has several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1157650420/vuelta-a-palencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta a Palencia&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 13.1 km route with minimal elevation gain. The paths along the Canal de Castilla also offer gentle, mostly flat terrain suitable for all skill levels.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Palencia?
A: Absolutely. The Montaña Palentina Natural Park in the north is known for its challenging routes with significant elevation gains, reaching heights above 1,600 meters. These high-mountain trails are designed to test endurance and technique, offering a rewarding experience for advanced mountain bikers.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass by natural attractions or viewpoints in Palencia?
A: Yes, many routes in Palencia offer scenic views and pass by natural attractions. The Montaña Palentina features spectacular natural surroundings, including glacial lakes and prominent peaks. Key viewpoints like Piedrasluengas offer breathtaking panoramic views of the Picos de Europa. You can also find natural wonders like the Tejeda de Tosande (an ancient yew grove) and the Covalagua Waterfall, which can be incorporated into your rides.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Palencia?
A: Yes, Palencia offers several circular mountain bike routes. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1157684271/parque-de-valdellano-circular-desde-palencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de Valdellano — circular desde Palencia&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.3 km trail exploring varied terrain. Another great choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318383729/palencia-a-husillos-circular-por-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia a Husillos — circular por Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the historic canal. The Montaña Palentina also features multi-stage circular routes for immersive experiences.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Palencia?
A: For families, the paths along the historic Canal de Castilla are an excellent choice. They are mostly flat and offer scenic, easy riding, allowing cyclists to adjust the distance to suit all family members. The green spaces along the Carrión River near the city of Palencia also provide leisurely cycling opportunities.

Q: Are mountain bike trails in Palencia dog-friendly?
A: While many outdoor areas in Palencia are generally welcoming to dogs, it's always best to check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially within protected areas like the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. When riding with your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry waste bags.

Q: What are the parking options for mountain biking trailheads in Palencia?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In and around the city of Palencia, you'll find various public parking options. For routes in more rural or natural park areas, designated parking lots are often available near popular starting points. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for recommended parking locations.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain bike trails in Palencia?
A: Public transport options to access trailheads can be limited, especially for routes deep within the Montaña Palentina. However, local bus services might connect to towns closer to some trail starting points. For routes originating directly from Palencia city, access is straightforward. Bringing your own vehicle is often the most flexible option for reaching diverse trailheads across the province.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Palencia?
A: The komoot community highly rates mountain biking in Palencia, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from the challenging mountain terrain to the serene, flat paths along the Canal de Castilla. The opportunity to combine cycling with exploring historical sites and natural wonders is also frequently highlighted.
tips: This beautiful dirt path runs between Husillos and Palencia. It is perfect for mountain biking and even gravel biking.

A bridge of Roman origin, renovated in the 15th century, which was an important enclave in the development of road and commercial networks from the Vaccean period to the beginning of the 20th century. There are no remains of its Roman origin, but its layout from medieval times. Currently, pilgrimages and medieval markets are held in its surroundings.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The Cuesta Verde circular route is designed to be completed in approximately 1 hour and 52 minutes, though this can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. This generally means it requires good fitness and some advanced riding skills. You can expect varied terrain, potentially including some gentle climbs and forest tracks, but it's not typically in the high-mountain, highly technical category.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location near Palencia, you can expect a mix of rural landscapes, potentially including forest tracks and paths along waterways. The region is known for its varied terrain, from flatter sections to gentle climbs, offering views of the countryside and possibly some historical elements.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features a couple of interesting points. You'll cross the Husillos–Palencia Path Bridge and the historic Puente de Puentecillas, both offering scenic spots.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll ride parts of the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur), GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal Sur), Vía Verde del Tren Burra, Camino de Figueldo, Camino de la Miranda, Camino de Nuestra Señora, Paseo de la Acequia, and Camino de San Antón.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cuesta Verde mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for 'Cuesta Verde' isn't detailed, as the route starts from Palencia, you would typically look for public parking options within or on the outskirts of the city that allow for easy access to the trailhead. Consider areas near the start point indicated on the komoot map.

faqs.question: Is the trailhead accessible by public transport?
faqs.answer: As the route begins in Palencia, it is generally well-connected by public transport. You can likely reach the starting point within the city using local bus services or by train to Palencia station, followed by a short ride to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking routes in rural areas of Palencia are dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or on shared paths. Please ensure you carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This ensures comfortable riding and good trail conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not typically need a permit or have to pay any entrance fees to ride the Cuesta Verde circular mountain bike route. Most trails in the Palencia region are freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate mountain bike ride like this?
faqs.answer: For a moderate mountain bike ride, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, some snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and appropriate cycling attire. A helmet is essential, and sunglasses and sunscreen are recommended, especially on sunny days.
